title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns, Setting Stage for New Leadership
subtitle: After two years in office, the British leader steps down as the country prepares for its seventh prime minister in a decade.

summary.en: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as leader of the Labour Party. He will remain in his role as a caretaker until a successor is chosen in late July. His departure follows low approval ratings and pressure from within his own party. Former mayor Andy Burnham is currently the leading candidate to take his place. International leaders have responded to the news with a mix of praise and political commentary.
summary.zh: 英國首相施凱爾宣布辭去工黨黨魁職務。他將留任看守首相，直到七月下旬選出繼任者為止。他的離職源於低迷的支持率以及來自黨內的壓力。前市長伯納姆目前是接任的最熱門人選。國際領袖對此消息的反應褒貶不一，並伴隨相關政治評論。

paragraphs.en: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as leader of the United Kingdom's governing Labour Party. This decision will lead to the appointment of the country's seventh prime minister in just ten years. According to NPR News, Starmer will stay on as a caretaker prime minister—a temporary leader who manages daily government tasks—until his party selects a new person for the job. He has asked his party to begin the process of choosing a new leader on July 9.
paragraphs.zh: 英國首相施凱爾 (Keir Starmer) 已宣布辭去執政黨工黨黨魁一職。這項決定將導致該國在短短十年內選出第七位首相。根據美國國家公共廣播電台 (NPR) 報導，施凱爾將留任看守首相——即負責處理政府日常事務的臨時領導人——直到工黨選出接任人選為止。他已要求黨內於七月九日啟動新任黨魁的遴選程序。

paragraphs.en: The Labour Party won a large majority in the U.K. Parliament two years ago. However, Starmer’s popularity has dropped to a very low level recently. NPR News reports that many voters feel he failed to make real improvements after 14 years of budget cuts under the previous Conservative Party government. Additionally, Starmer faced criticism for his decision to appoint Peter Mandelson, a friend of the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, as the British ambassador to Washington. With a new anti-immigration party gaining support in recent polls, members of the Labour Party began calling for Starmer to leave his position.
paragraphs.zh: 工黨在兩年前的英國國會大選中贏得壓倒性多數。然而，施凱爾近期的民調支持度卻降至極低水準。NPR 報導指出，許多選民認為他在前任保守黨政府長達十四年的預算削減政策後，並未做出實質改善。此外，施凱爾因任命已故性犯罪者艾普斯坦 (Jeffrey Epstein) 的友人曼德森 (Peter Mandelson) 為英國駐華盛頓大使而飽受批評。隨著一個反移民的新興政黨在近期民調中獲得支持，工黨成員開始要求施凱爾下台。

paragraphs.en: In a speech outside 10 Downing Street, Starmer explained that he had listened to his party members. "The question my party is asking now, is whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election," Starmer said. "I have heard the answer of my parliamentary party to that question, and I accept the answer with good grace." Despite the criticism, Starmer defended his record, pointing to increased spending on defense and healthcare, as well as a decrease in the number of people entering the U.K. without official permission.
paragraphs.zh: 在唐寧街十號外發表的演說中，施凱爾解釋他已傾聽黨內成員的心聲。施凱爾表示：「我黨目前提出的問題是，我是否為帶領我們進入下屆大選的最佳人選。我已聽取國會黨團對此問題的答案，並欣然接受這個結果。」儘管面臨批評，施凱爾仍為自己的政績辯護，指出國防與醫療支出的增加，以及未經官方許可進入英國的人數有所減少。

paragraphs.en: It appears that the transition of power will be quick. Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester, has confirmed he will run for the leadership. According to NPR News, another potential candidate, Wes Streeting, has already said he will support Burnham. This means Burnham could potentially become the next prime minister without facing a contest. If this happens, he could enter office by late July.
paragraphs.zh: 權力交接似乎將會迅速完成。前大曼徹斯特市長伯納姆 (Andy Burnham) 已證實將角逐黨魁之位。根據 NPR 報導，另一位潛在候選人史特林 (Wes Streeting) 已表示將支持伯納姆。這意味著伯納姆可能無需經過競爭便能成為下一任首相。若情況如此，他最快可能在七月下旬就職。

paragraphs.en: The resignation has caused changes on the international stage. The Guardian reports that the European Union and the United Kingdom have decided to postpone an upcoming summit—a high-level meeting between government leaders—that was planned for July 22. European Council President António Costa stated that they are now reassessing the situation, hoping that the next British leader will continue to work on improving the relationship between the U.K. and the EU.
paragraphs.zh: 這場辭職風波已在國際舞台引發變動。《衛報》報導，歐盟與英國已決定推遲原定於七月二十二日舉行的高層政府領袖峰會。歐洲理事會主席柯斯塔 (António Costa) 表示，他們目前正在重新評估局勢，希望下一任英國領導人能持續致力於改善英國與歐盟的關係。

paragraphs.en: Global leaders have shared their thoughts on Starmer’s departure. Ursula von der Leyen, the president of the European Commission, thanked Starmer for his work, noting that European and Ukrainian security is stronger because of his leadership. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy also expressed his gratitude for Starmer’s support during his time in office. However, not all reactions were supportive. Former U.S. President Donald Trump, who has had a complicated relationship with the British government, criticized Starmer’s handling of immigration and energy policies in a statement on his social media platform.
paragraphs.zh: 全球領袖紛紛對施凱爾的離職發表看法。歐盟執委會主席馮德萊恩 (Ursula von der Leyen) 感謝施凱爾的貢獻，並指出歐洲與烏克蘭的安全因他的領導而更加穩固。烏克蘭總統澤倫斯基 (Volodymyr Zelenskyy) 也對施凱爾在任期間的支持表達感謝。然而，並非所有反應皆為正面。曾與英國政府關係複雜的前美國總統川普 (Donald Trump)，則在其社群媒體平台上發文，批評施凱爾處理移民與能源政策的方式。

paragraphs.en: This leadership change comes at a difficult time for the United Kingdom. Tuesday marks the 10th anniversary of the Brexit referendum, the vote where the British public decided to leave the European Union. John McTernan, a former political advisor, told NPR News that the country has faced significant economic damage since that decision. This economic struggle has led to public anger, which has helped new, smaller political parties gain popularity. Reform UK, a party focused on stopping immigration, has seen success in recent local elections, which has caused concern for the traditional Labour Party.
paragraphs.zh: 此次領導層更迭正值英國的艱難時刻。週二適逢英國脫歐公投十週年，當時英國民眾投票決定脫離歐盟。前政治顧問麥特南 (John McTernan) 向 NPR 表示，自該決定以來，英國已面臨嚴重的經濟損害。這種經濟困境引發民怨，助長了新興小型政黨的聲勢。專注於遏止移民的「英國改革黨」(Reform UK) 在近期地方選舉中取得成功，這已引起傳統工黨的擔憂。

paragraphs.en: As the U.K. prepares for this change, the focus remains on whether the new leadership can address the country's economic challenges and internal political divisions. While Starmer’s time as prime minister has ended, the impact of his policies and the future direction of the Labour Party remain subjects of intense debate. For now, the country waits to see if Andy Burnham will take the lead and how he will address the concerns of a nation that has seen frequent changes in government over the last decade.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著英國準備迎接這項變動，焦點仍集中在新領導層能否解決國家的經濟挑戰與內部政治分歧。儘管施凱爾的首相任期已告一段落，但他政策的影響以及工黨未來的走向，仍是激烈辯論的主題。目前，全國都在等待伯納姆是否會接下重擔，以及他將如何回應這個在過去十年內頻繁更換政府的國家所面臨的種種憂慮。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To continue to remain in a job or position for a period of time after one might have been expected to leave.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於日常對話，此處指在辭職後繼續暫代職務。文中：Starmer will stay on as a caretaker prime minister—a temporary leader who manages daily government tasks—until his party selects a new person for the job.

keyTerms.definitionEn: In the most suitable or advantageous position to do something.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 最有能力；處於最有利的位置
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡的 placed 並非指「放置」，而是指「處於某種狀態或地位」。文中：The question my party is asking now, is whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election

keyTerms.definitionEn: To assume control or become the person in charge.
keyTerms.noteZh: 容易誤以為是「領先」，但在這裡指接管領導權。文中：For now, the country waits to see if Andy Burnham will take the lead and how he will address the concerns of a nation that has seen frequent changes in government over the last decade.

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: Keir Starmer has resigned, paving way for a 7th U.K. prime minister in 10 years

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: European leaders pay tribute to Starmer as EU-UK summit is postponed

quiz.question: What specific action did Keir Starmer take regarding the timeline for selecting his successor?

options.A: He immediately stepped down to allow an election on July 9.
options.B: He requested that the Labour Party begin the selection process on July 9.
options.C: He confirmed that a new leader would be officially appointed by July 9.
options.D: He announced he would remain in office until the next general election.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段最後一句：「He has asked his party to begin the process of choosing a new leader on July 9.」，明確指出他要求政黨在 7 月 9 日開始遴選程序，而非當天完成。選項 A 錯誤，因為他會先擔任看守首相；選項 C 錯誤，文中未提及 7 月 9 日是任命完成日；選項 D 錯誤，他將在選出新領導人後卸任，而非留任至大選。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why might Andy Burnham become the next prime minister without a formal leadership contest?

options.A: Because the Labour Party has cancelled all future leadership elections.
options.B: Because he is the only person qualified to manage the country's economic crisis.
options.C: Because other potential rivals are choosing to support his candidacy instead of running.
options.D: Because Starmer personally selected him as his preferred replacement.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。第四段提到 Wes Streeting 已經表示會支持 Burnham，且文中提到這意味著 Burnham 可能在沒有競爭對手的情況下當選。這暗示了其他潛在候選人若選擇支持而非競爭，將導致無競爭的局面。選項 A 錯誤，文中未提及取消選舉；選項 B 錯誤，文中未評估其資格；選項 D 錯誤，文中並未提到 Starmer 指定接班人。

quiz.question: In the context of the fifth paragraph, what is the meaning of the word 'summit'?

options.A: The highest peak of a mountain range.
options.B: A formal meeting between high-level government leaders.
options.C: A final deadline for making a difficult decision.
options.D: An agreement to end a political disagreement.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第五段提到：「The Guardian reports that the European Union and the United Kingdom have decided to postpone an upcoming summit—a high-level meeting between government leaders...」。括號內的解釋直接定義了此處的 summit 指的是高層政府官員的會議。選項 A 是該詞的字面義（山頂），但在文中不適用；選項 C 與 D 雖與政治相關，但非文中對該詞的定義。

quiz.question: What is the central focus of this article regarding the current state of the United Kingdom?

options.A: A detailed analysis of the economic failure of the Brexit referendum.
options.B: A biography of Keir Starmer's political career and his personal life.
options.C: The international impact of Donald Trump's opinions on British politics.
options.D: The resignation of Keir Starmer and the resulting political uncertainty for the U.K.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。全文主要圍繞 Keir Starmer 辭職的消息，並探討了此舉對英國國內政治（如接班人問題、政黨壓力）及國際關係（如與歐盟的關係）的影響，反映了英國政局的不確定性。選項 A 僅是背景之一；選項 B 並非傳記；選項 C 僅是文章末段提到的眾多國際反應之一，非主旨。
